1

Personal
Professional
Development

26th May 2024
Subject: PPD
Hand-in portfolio

HBO-ICT
Joeri Harreman

2

Table of Contents
Role and context in the assignment (1) ........................................................................................................................ 4
Student check: ............................................................................................................................................................ 4
Collected proof: ..................................................................................................................................................... 4

3

Collected proof: .....................................................................................................................................................11
Appendixes ................................................................................................................................................................... 12

4

Role and context in the assignment

Student check: 

Collected proof:
When I started this year, I was used to the amount of work from the secondary education. I thought it would be the same, but o was I wrong. I had a lot of problems trying to keep up with the problem-solving sessions we had after classes. I was thinking I could do everything on my own to show that I am capable of doing things. I was scared to ask questions and this lead to insecurity . This lack of structure and self-consciousness didn’t help me at all. I noticed I needed to do something about it.

I took action by incorporating short breaks into my problem-solving sessions. These breaks provided me with the opportunity to recharge and gain a fresh perspective. Additionally, I began reflecting on past experiences to refine my problem-solving methods. By analyzing successes and failures, I could adapt and improve my approach.

Later on, I noticed a significant improvement in my problem-solving abilities as I became more comfortable seeking assistance. Peers and instructors provided positive feedback on my collaborative efforts, reinforcing the value of teamwork.

Moving forward, I understand the importance of offering constructive feedback and recognition for collaborative endeavors. This fosters a supportive environment that encourages everyone to contribute their best. I now realize that continuous reflection and adaptation are essential for personal growth.

See appendix 1: Schedule showcasing breaks integrated into problem-solving sessions.

5

Openminded to different opinions

When I started, I realized I wasn't very open to diverse ideas, which limited how I solved problems. My growth lies in understanding the importance of embracing different perspectives.

To actively change this, I began listening more attentively and engaging in discussions to understand different viewpoints better. I also conducted more research on various opinions, which helped me understand what others want and prevented me from judging people's opinions too quickly.

Later on, I realized that feedback is crucial for growth. It allows me to see things from different angles and make significant improvements. With constructive feedback, I can adapt my projects to better meet the needs of clients, for example. It also deepens my understanding of diverse perspectives.

You can see this change in my active participation in discussions and my regular requests for feedback.

Moving forward, I will continue participating in discussions and seeking feedback to show progress. This way, I can keep learning and better understand different viewpoints.

See appendix 2: What I did with feedback from our client ( implementing feedback). Active group discussions and listening to other opinions.
Identifying Problems

When I started, I struggled to see problems as challenges I could overcome. But now, I understand that my growth lies in cultivating discipline to approach problems differently.

So, I began to change my approach. When I came to the HZ, everything shifted from pen and paper to exclusively using my computer. I realized that I don't work as neatly or efficiently without pen and paper. Consequently, I returned to writing things down, whether on whiteboards or in notebooks—everywhere. Furthermore I notices whenever I can’t figure something out I immediately go to a teacher or a peer. I acknowledged this and started working on this, I started watching more video’s about the problems and also writing them down.

Later on, it came up to me that I couldn’t keep track of what I need to do by the end of the school year. By writing down everything in my notebook. I could keep track of what I needed to do and it gave me more structure for my “to-do’s”.

Looking ahead, I know I need to keep practicing these strategies. Asking for help does not make me a worse person. It helps me grown and I need to keep this in mind. Because you can’t do everything on your own.
See appendix 3: Using pen and paper, Whiteboards. And doing research with video material on problems.

6

Various Solutions

Student check: 

When I started the Programming Basics course, I often found myself stuck on assignments that seemed to have no clear solution. My growth lies in developing the ability to explore multiple solutions for these problems.

I used a variety of methods to approach these assignments. First, I researched different programming techniques and algorithms that could apply to the problem at hand. I also consulted online forums and tutorials to see how others approached similar issues.

Later, I began to experiment with different coding strategies on my own. For one particularly challenging assignment, I tried three different algorithms before finding one that worked efficiently. I also regularly discussed my approaches with classmates and teachers, which provided new insights and alternative methods.

Moving forward, I now know I have to keep exploring multiple solutions and actively seek feedback to enhance my problem-solving skills. This iterative process of trying different methods, seeking input, and refining my approach has been invaluable in my development.

See appendix 4: Screenshot of my notes outlining the problem and initial ideas, Screenshot of my code attempts for three different algorithms, Screenshot of feedback from my teacher and classmates.
Planning & Monitoring ahead

My starting point was realizing my planning issue when feedback highlighted my frequent forgetfulness. Despite being aware, I hadn't actively addressed it until PPD encouraged me to take action.

I began using the mobile app reminders, which notifies me whenever I set a reminder. This significantly reduced my forgetfulness by keeping track of important tasks. Additionally, I incorporated the use of a whiteboard in my room for weekly planning. Every Sunday, I outline my tasks for the week ahead, ensuring I stay organized Furthermore, I synchronized my Outlook calendar with my school calendar to consolidate all my appointments in one place. This was crucial since I tend to check only one source, often overlooking notes on other activities .

Later on, these methods proved effective, not only in enhancing my organization but also in relieving stress and significantly reducing my forgetfulness. And not to forget my family and friends really appreciated that I stopped forgetting so much.

Moving forward, I will continue to employ these strategies. Now recognizing their effectiveness, I see a clear path toward a more organized life and happy people around me, which, in turn, minimizes stress and improves my memory.

See appendix 5: Outlook calendar and reminders app

7

Feasibility of Tasks

When I realized I needed help, I was afraid to ask. I worried I might seem unsure of myself and didn't know if my questions were good enough. But I knew I had to overcome this fear to get better.

So, I started studying with my friends. We worked together on assignments and exams. This made it easier to ask questions because we were all in the same boat. I didn't have to ask for help as much because we were helping each other.

When I studied alone, I reached out to my student assistant. I'd message them on Teams or go see them in the study area.
And when I needed help with databases, I asked Frans. He helped me a lot without making me feel dumb. Also I’m lucky enough to have help from home. Whenever I couldn’t figure something out in the weekends and I don’t want to disturb teachers and classmates, I could just ask my dad.

Getting positive feedback from everyone after asking questions made me feel more confident. Now, I'm not afraid to ask for help. I know people support me, and asking questions is how I learn and grow. Also it helps that people ask questions back. This gave me a confident boost and helped me moving forward with asking questions.

See appendix 6: Asking questions to teachers, student assistants and friends. Also working with my friends and asking questions
Motivation and providing support

When I started this course, I had a lot of initial motivation, primarily driven by my dream to eventually work at my father's place. However, I found myself lacking motivation in certain areas, particularly in game development, where I struggled to find intrinsic drive.

To address this lack of motivation, I found that circumstances often pushed me to work despite my initial reluctance.
When faced with tasks I didn't enjoy, I reminded myself of my overarching goals: to pass the year cum-laude and obtain my propaedeutic certificate. These goals were significant to me as they represented both personal achievement and the desire to make my family proud. I sometimes think I set my own goals too high, but then I think that it is okay to set high goals, as long as there are more reachable goals in between. This helps me having a good feeling of work I do, eventough I don’t reach my high stretched goals.

Despite challenges, my determination to achieve these goals kept me motivated, even when working on projects I didn't particularly enjoy. For instance, in a group project on Object-Oriented Programming (OOP), I faced difficulties when my teammates lacked motivation. I contemplated whether to take on all tasks myself or encourage my teammates to contribute, ultimately opting for the latter as I prioritized achieving good grades.

Moving forward, I recognize that not everyone shares the same level of motivation, and I need to adapt accordingly. While
I continue to work towards my propaedeutic certificate, achieving cum-laude requires further effort. With a current average of 7.6, I understand the need to work harder, particularly in areas where I struggle. I believe that with dedication and collaboration in future projects, I can improve my grades and continue striving towards my goals.

See appendix 7: My average grade. Also the OOP project commits to show I did a lot.
8

Study Choice and personal

I recognized my tendency to postpone studying at home, which often resulted in not completing tasks or doing them too late. Additionally, I acknowledged a broader uncertainty regarding my future direction, realizing that I’m not entirely sure what I want to pursue after school.

To address this, I adopted a more proactive approach by waking up at 6 for swimming, demonstrating a commitment to positive change and a disciplined schedule . Part of my strategy to address my uncertainty about the future involved actively researching various companies and industries. This exploration is aimed at broadening my understanding of potential career paths and aligning my interests with my future endeavors .

Understanding that being at home increases my likelihood of procrastination, I planned to extend my stay at school postclasses to focus on assignments. But this doesn’t mean I am not working at home. I will strive to have my focus at home and adapt my work environment. Furthermore, my interest in certain companies—especially our customers for the Laravel project, which operates in a similar field to my dad's company—has heightened my enthusiasm for this work. Recognizing a strong alignment with my interests and career aspirations, this engagement provides a valuable perspective on my future direction.

Moving forward, while I am committed to utilizing school time for assignments and collaborative study sessions with friends and classmates, my exploration into companies related to my field of interest adds another layer of motivation.
The Laravel project, in particular, represents not only an immediate academic challenge but also a potential stepping stone for my professional development. My involvement with a company that mirrors my dad’s business has sparked a deeper interest in pursuing a similar career path. This project could significantly influence my future choices, although I remain open to evaluating how this experience shapes my interests and goals in the long term.

See appendix 8: Swimming, and getting information from companies. Work environment at home.

9

ICT Jobs and Ambitions

When I started my journey in ICT, I thought I possessed a good foundation of knowledge. When starting the year I quickly found out that was not the case. I acknowledged the necessity of studying and finding more out about the program itself.
The foundation I had is good enough to have the right thinking. But this doesn’t mean that I can do this with 2 fingers in my nose. This is why I researched a lot more about companies and what skills you need to have.

Instead of relying solely on traditional resources like online courses and academic journals, I tapped into the wealth of knowledge available at my dad's company. Sitting alongside the experienced professionals there, many of whom were former students from our school, I absorbed practical insights and asked questions to deepen my understanding.

As time progressed, my learning approach evolved. Because of the help from the colleagues there, my knowledge expanded. Hench why my learning approach was way different than before. By knowing a lot more about what later is needed to really be an good business person in the IT industry. Independence and Willpower is an really important part of the IT industry. This is why I started making my own projects, my latest one is apple or cheesecake, with the data from an energy company that give the day ahead prices of the energy market. It will show if the prices are low or if the prices are high. When you can bake a apple pie or cheesecake the cheapest.

Looking ahead, I am committed to maintaining this proactive learning approach. I understand the dynamic nature of the
ICT field and the importance of adaptability. Therefore, I pledge to continue seeking opportunities for growth, leveraging both formal education and practical experience to excel in the ever-evolving landscape of ICT.

See appendix 9: getting information at my dad’s company . Making my own projects.

10

Role and context of the assignment

When I started this year, my understanding of Agile roles such as Team Coach, OSC, Tracker, and Developer was minimal.
I knew these roles existed but lacked detailed knowledge about their responsibilities and how they contribute to a project.

To improve my understanding, I participating in our group projects, where we had to use the agile roles. In the project
FPR1/2 we use the agile roles and switched every 2 weeks from role to role. I noticed that some roles are harder to do and some are easier but by doing these roles I really noticed what my weaknesses are strong points are. When I noticed my weaknesses I started working on improving these positions. For example the team coach was a different thing for me, I’ve never been really a guy that keeps track of what everybody does and assign then roles. But I needed to overcome this and do my role properly.

I kept track of everything by checking up on my group mates. Texted them when something was wrong and tried to check up on everyone. I’ve not had a other massive role as team coach, but this will come in the end of the project. My group says they have faith in me to control a lot of things in the end of the project. That’s why I’ve been a developer, wikipage updater, readme file updater and a on-site customer. These roles are pretty easy to do and for me weren’t that hard.

Moving forward, I know I have to go out of my comfort zone and do the things that are needed to do. Such as saying to people that they are doing something wrong or lacking behind on group work. I also will be glad to take bigger roles to improve my insecurities and my skills to lead or track a team.

See appendix 10: Checking up on my peers, Roles I had in group projects, doing my role.
Methodical approach

When I started this year, my approach to task management was rather unstructured. I often completed tasks as they came to mind, without considering their priority or logical sequence. This is something I took with from the secondary school, I just did what needed to be done in a un-logical order and without structure.

To improve, I began using task management tools such as Trello and Notion. I started by listing all tasks related to my projects and then organizing them based on priority and deadlines. For example, in the OOP project, I created a Trello board with columns for “To Do,” “In Progress,” and “Done.” I further prioritized tasks within each column by due date and importance. I did the same for the FPR project, I made a notion page where I structure my tasks my data and what needs to be done first.

Moving forward, I know I have to continue refining my prioritization skills by experimenting with different tools and methods, such as the Eisenhower Matrix, to further enhance my efficiency and effectiveness in task management.

See appendix 11: Trello board for OOP, Notion page with deadlines and priority, Eisenhower matrix in my notion page.

11

Purposeful Communication

When I started this year, I knew that effective communication was essential but I knew limited experience in this subject.
For me it is hard to send my messages to specific audiences or in contexts.

I began by working on assignments that required clear and targeted communication. For example, in the PCO course, I created a personal website to express my enthusiasm for the ICT program. I focused on ensuring that the content was engaging and relevant to my target audience—my teachers. I also participated in a group project where we created an interactive educational game for primary school pupils ( 8-11 y/o ). We carefully considered the language and instructions to ensure they were age-appropriate and engaging for the pupils. We also made our own feedback paper to gather feedback from our target audience. This was to see if they understood the language we where writing. I also have the FPR project were we are working with a client ( Stedin ). For me it was hard to picture what they really wanted as an result. We as a group had a lot of brainstorming sessions, but also a lot to show for. Such as, we created prototypes, designs and more. We showed the client to get a really good picture of what they exactly want. These brainstorming sessions helped me to improve my ability to ask more specific questions and to adapt my thoughts.

I still have to improve my ability to adapt quickly to different communication contexts. Moving forward, I plan to engage in more client-facing projects and seek feedback on my communication style to enhance my adaptability and effectiveness.

See appendix 12: Screenshots of my personal website and the interactive game, along with feedback from my teachers and peers, demonstrate my progress in purposeful.

12

Appendixes

To address this lack of motivation, I found that circumstances often pushed me to work despite my initial reluctance.
When faced with tasks I didn't enjoy, I reminded myself of my overarching goals: to pass the year cum-laude and obtain
my propaedeutic certificate. These goals were significant to me as they represented both personal achievement and the
desire to make my family proud. I sometimes think I set my own goals too high, but then I think that it is okay to set high
goals, as long as there are more reachable goals in between. This helps me having a good feeling of work I do, eventough I
don’t reach my high stretched goals.

Despite challenges, my determination to achieve these goals kept me motivated, even when working on projects I didn't
particularly enjoy. For instance, in a group project on Object-Oriented Programming (OOP), I faced difficulties when my
teammates lacked motivation. I contemplated whether to take on all tasks myself or encourage my teammates to
contribute, ultimately opting for the latter as I prioritized achieving good grades.

Moving forward, I recognize that not everyone shares the same level of motivation, and I need to adapt accordingly. While
I continue to work towards my propaedeutic certificate, achieving cum-laude requires further effort. With a current
average of 7.6, I understand the need to work harder, particularly in areas where I struggle. I believe that with dedication
and collaboration in future projects, I can improve my grades and continue striving towards my goals.

See appendix 7: My average grade. Also the OOP project commits to show I did a lot.

actively researching various companies and industries. This exploration is aimed at broadening my understanding of

project, which operates in a similar field to my dad's company—has heightened my enthusiasm for this work. Recognizing
a strong alignment with my interests and career aspirations, this engagement provides a valuable perspective on my future

friends and classmates, my exploration into companies related to my field of interest adds another layer of motivation.
The Laravel project, in particular, represents not only an immediate academic challenge but also a potential stepping
stone for my professional development. My involvement with a company that mirrors my dad’s business has sparked a

Methodical approach

Student check: 

Collected proof:

When I started this year, my approach to task management was rather unstructured. I often completed tasks as they came
to mind, without considering their priority or logical sequence. This is something I took with from the secondary school, I
just did what needed to be done in a un-logical order and without structure.

To improve, I began using task management tools such as Trello and Notion. I started by listing all tasks related to my
projects and then organizing them based on priority and deadlines. For example, in the OOP project, I created a Trello
board with columns for “To Do,” “In Progress,” and “Done.” I further prioritized tasks within each column by due date and
importance. I did the same for the FPR project, I made a notion page where I structure my tasks my data and what needs
to be done first.

Moving forward, I know I have to continue refining my prioritization skills by experimenting with different tools and
methods, such as the Eisenhower Matrix, to further enhance my efficiency and effectiveness in task management.

See appendix 11: Trello board for OOP, Notion page with deadlines and priority, Eisenhower matrix in my notion page.

engaging and relevant to my target audience—my teachers. I also participated in a group project where we created an
interactive educational game for primary school pupils ( 8-11 y/o ). We carefully considered the language and instructions
to ensure they were age-appropriate and engaging for the pupils. We also made our own feedback paper to gather
feedback from our target audience. This was to see if they understood the language we where writing. I also have the FPR
project were we are working with a client ( Stedin ). For me it was hard to picture what they really wanted as an result. We
as a group had a lot of brainstorming sessions, but also a lot to show for. Such as, we created prototypes, designs and
more. We showed the client to get a really good picture of what they exactly want. These brainstorming sessions helped me
to improve my ability to ask more specific questions and to adapt my thoughts.

I still have to improve my ability to adapt quickly to different communication contexts. Moving forward, I plan to engage
in more client-facing projects and seek feedback on my communication style to enhance my adaptability and effectiveness.

See appendix 12: Screenshots of my personal website and the interactive game, along with feedback from my teachers and
peers, demonstrate my progress in purposeful.

solution.

algorithms that could apply to the problem at hand.

I also consulted online forums and tutorials to see how others
approached similar issues.
Later, I began to experiment with different coding strategies on my own.

For one particularly challenging assignment, I
tried three different algorithms before finding one that worked efficiently.

I also regularly discussed my approaches with
classmates and teachers, which provided new insights and alternative methods.

Moving forward, I now know I have to keep exploring multiple solutions and actively seek feedback to enhance my
problem-solving skills.

This iterative process of trying different methods, seeking input, and refining my approach has
been invaluable in my development.

Planning & Monitoring ahead

Student check: 

Collected proof:
My starting point was realizing my planning issue when feedback highlighted my frequent forgetfulness.

Despite being
aware, I hadn't actively addressed it until PPD encouraged me to take action.
I began using the mobile app reminders, which notifies me whenever I set a reminder.

This significantly reduced my
forgetfulness by keeping track of important tasks.

Additionally, I incorporated the use of a whiteboard in my room for
weekly planning.

Every Sunday, I outline my tasks for the week ahead, ensuring I stay organized Furthermore, I
synchronized my Outlook calendar with my school calendar to consolidate all my appointments in one place.

This was
crucial since I tend to check only one source, often overlooking notes on other activities .

Later on, these methods proved effective, not only in enhancing my organization but also in relieving stress and
significantly reducing my forgetfulness.

And not to forget my family and friends really appreciated that I stopped
forgetting so much.
Moving forward, I will continue to employ these strategies.

Now recognizing their effectiveness, I see a clear path toward a
more organized life and happy people around me, which, in turn, minimizes stress and improves my memory.
